sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c: no data
Next Step history 2026-05-20 to 2026-07-01: procedural (registration, await tender, RFT submission) with no pain or ROI articulated
Zero Avoma calls (partner-led channel)
Inference: RFT context implies procurement modernization intent, but pain/metrics remain unvalidated
metrics.narrative: No quantified business problem, ROI target, cost reduction goal, or operational pain metric documented across Salesforce fields or Next Step updates. The RFT invitation implies organizational need (Bank Rakyat seeks a procurement/contract/sourcing platform), but the specific pain (manual procurement cycle time, incumbent system technical debt, compliance/audit risk, supplier consolidation savings target, invoice processing backlog) and the pain owner are unknown. Business_Objectives__c, Customer_Business_Problem__c, Value_to_Customer__c, and Compelling_Event__c fields were not populated in this org or returned no data. Without a validated business case anchored to Strategy priorities, the RFT response risks missing the highest-weighted evaluation criteria and failing to resonate with Economic Buyer and board-level sponsors. The recommended move is to engage the Economic Buyer (CFO candidate from account contacts) or Strategy lead (Firdaus Faudzi, Chief Strategy and Transformation Officer) through the partner channel to surface and quantify the business case before RFT submission.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: Syiadatul (Procurement Executive, Evaluator) — no Champion/Executive Sponsor role
SF X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c: no data
Next Step 2026-06-01: 'good relationships built' (generic, no named champion or advocacy evidence)
Next Step 2026-07-01: partner building BOD relationship (indirect, external to Bank Rakyat)
Zero Avoma calls, zero evidence of internal advocacy
champion.narrative: No champion identified. Syiadatul Nazlia Mohd Hassim (Procurement Executive, Evaluator OpportunityContactRole) is the sole buyer-side contact, an operational/technical evaluator with no confirmed advocacy, no access to Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, and no evidence of pushing Zycus internally vs competitors. X10b_Champion_Business_Buyer__c, Decision_Maker_Name_Title__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c fields returned no data. Next Step updates reference 'good relationships built' (2026-06-01) but do not name a champion or describe advocacy actions (introducing Zycus to senior stakeholders, sharing internal evaluation docs, blocking competitors, driving timeline urgency). Zero Avoma calls to observe champion behavior. The partner is building BOD-level relationship through a bumiputra partner (2026-07-01), which may create indirect sponsorship, but no named internal champion is confirmed. Without a champion, Zycus is blind to the evaluation committee's internal preferences, competitor positioning, scoring dynamics, and political/functional objections. The recommended move is to develop Syiadatul as a coach (ask her to share evaluation timeline, committee composition, competitor shortlist, and scoring criteria) and request the partner introduce Zycus to a senior sponsor (EVP Procurement, Chief Strategy Officer) who can champion the deal at board level.

sources: SF Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c: no data
Next Step updates: no competitor named
Zero Tasks referencing competitors
Zero Avoma calls
Inference: APAC bank RFTs typically shortlist SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, but Bank Rakyat's shortlist unconfirmed
competition.narrative: Zero competitive intelligence. No competitor identified in Salesforce fields (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, Existing_vendor__c returned no data or were not present), no competitor named in Next Step updates, no competitive mentions in Tasks, and zero Avoma calls (partner-led channel). It is unknown whether Bank Rakyat is replacing an incumbent S2P/contract platform (SAP Ariba, Coupa, Oracle, Ivalua, local APAC vendor), running a greenfield selection, or benchmarking Zycus against a do-nothing/manual baseline. APAC bank tenders typically shortlist 3-5 vendors (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Zycus, regional players like Negometrix or MyEG are common in Malaysia). Without knowing the shortlist, incumbent advantage, competitor pricing, or peer vendor positioning (e.g. Ariba's ERP integration story, Coupa's user adoption narrative, GEP's cost efficiency), Zycus cannot differentiate the RFT response, price competitively, or tailor the Agentic AI wedge to beat a specific rival. The recommended move is to request the RFT shortlist and incumbent vendor name from Syiadatul or the partner before final submission, then position Zycus's Agentic AI and APAC bank reference portfolio as the differentiated wedge.

sources: SF Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Compelling_Event__c: no data
Next Step updates: no pain articulated
Zero Avoma calls
Inference: RFT context implies procurement modernization pain exists (else no tender), but specifics unvalidated
identify_pain.narrative: The specific operational or financial pain Bank Rakyat seeks to solve with a source-to-pay platform is undocumented. The RFT invitation and eight-product scope imply a broad procurement/contract/sourcing modernization intent, but the pain is not articulated: is it manual contract authoring/redlining cycle time, lack of supplier risk visibility, invoice processing backlog, maverick spend/non-compliance, procurement cost-per-transaction, or inability to negotiate supplier terms at scale? The pain owner (the executive/function suffering the impact) is likewise unknown. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c, Compelling_Event__c fields returned no data. Next Step updates are procedural (registration, tender wait, RFT submission) with no buyer-stated pain. Zero Avoma calls (partner-led). Without confirmed pain, Zycus cannot anchor the RFT response to the buyer's highest-priority problem, differentiate on pain-solution fit vs competitors, or build urgency with the Economic Buyer. The recommended move is to engage Syiadatul or a senior stakeholder (IT Head, Strategy Officer) through the partner to surface the pain and pain owner before final RFT submission, then tailor the Agentic AI and iContract/iRisk narrative to that pain.

sources: SF Current_Contract_Expiration__c: no data
Next Step updates: no mention of InfoSec, legal, MSA, SOW, or integration requirements
Zero Tasks referencing legal, InfoSec, or contracting
Inference: APAC bank = likely InfoSec/regulatory gate, but unconfirmed
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics, legal review, InfoSec questionnaire, ERP/system integration requirements, and MSA/SOW scoping are completely unknown. Current_Contract_Expiration__c returned no data, so it is unclear whether Bank Rakyat is replacing an incumbent (which would trigger data migration, integration handoff, and contract exit clause considerations) or greenfield. For an APAC bank, InfoSec review, data residency/sovereignty compliance, and regulatory audit (central bank, PDPA Malaysia) are typically mandatory and multi-week gates, but none are mentioned in Next Step updates or Salesforce fields. No Tasks reference legal, InfoSec, or SOW kickoff. The partner-led channel may be handling these workstreams independently, but without visibility Zycus cannot plan legal/InfoSec resource allocation, estimate contract close duration, or de-risk compliance blockers. The recommended move is to request the RFT's InfoSec/compliance appendix and contracting requirements from the partner, and confirm whether Bank Rakyat requires on-premise/private cloud deployment, data localization, or third-party audit certification.

sources: OpportunityContactRole: 1 buyer-side contact (Syiadatul, Procurement Executive, Evaluator) — no EB/Executive Sponsor role
SF X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c: no data
Next Step updates: no EB named
Account contacts: senior candidates present (CFO, EVP Procurement, Chief Strategy Officer titles) but no opp-level engagement confirmed
economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped and unengaged. Account-level contacts include a CFO candidate (contact data shows senior finance/procurement roles), but no OpportunityContactRole marks an Economic Buyer, no Next Step update names budget authority engagement, and zero Tasks or Avoma calls show EB participation. X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c fields returned no data or were not present. The deal is single-threaded to the relevant stakeholder Hassim (Procurement Executive, Evaluator role), a technical/operational evaluator with no budget sign-off authority for a $200k platform deal. For an APAC bank, the Economic Buyer is typically the CFO or a C-level procurement/transformation sponsor (EVP Procurement, Chief Strategy Officer). Without EB access before RFT evaluation, Zycus cannot validate budget availability, align the commercial proposal to board priorities, or de-risk pricing/ROI objections at final approval. The recommended move is to request the partner introduce Zycus to the budget owner (CFO or EVP Procurement candidate from account contacts) ahead of shortlist to validate commercial fit.

sources: Next Step 2026-07-01: RFP dates delayed to second half July, partner building BOD relationship
Next Step 2026-06-01: RFP due 15 June (now past, slipped)
Next Step 2026-05-20: vendor registration complete, await tender mid/late June
SF What_is_the_decision_process__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c: no data
Inference: RFT context confirms multi-stage formal procurement; bumiputra partner BOD engagement signals board-level approval step, but full process/timeline unconfirmed
decision_process.narrative: The decision process is RFT-driven (formal tender), confirming a structured, multi-stage bank procurement cycle, but the committee, approval chain, timeline, and shortlist-to-award milestones are unknown. The Next Step chronology shows: vendor registration completed 2026-05-20, original RFT deadline 2026-06-15, deadline slipped to second half of July 2026 per 2026-07-01 update. The partner is building Board of Directors (BOD) relationship through a bumiputra partner, signaling that final award approval may require board or executive committee sign-off. What_is_the_decision_process__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c fields returned no data. The evaluation committee composition (Procurement, IT, Risk, Finance, Strategy representation) is unconfirmed. The process steps AFTER RFT submission (evaluation duration, shortlist notification date, demo/presentation round, commercial negotiation round, reference checks, InfoSec review, contracting kickoff) are undocumented. Without a confirmed timeline, Zycus cannot plan demo/commercial/legal resources or pressure-test the 2026-12-31 close date. The recommended move is to request the RFT evaluation and award timeline from Syiadatul or the partner before final submission.

sources: SF Products__c: iContract;iSource;iSupplier;eProcurement;eInvoicing;iRequest;iRisk;Agentic AI (confirmed scope, implies functional coverage criterion)
Next Step 2026-05-20: received RFT invitation, team answering tender questions (implies criteria exist but not surfaced to Zycus)
SF Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c: no data
Inference: APAC bank RFTs typically weight compliance/InfoSec, regional references, pricing, and AI capability, but Bank Rakyat's specific weighting is unconfirmed
decision_criteria.narrative: RFT evaluation criteria are implied by the tender invitation and product scope (iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, eInvoicing, iRequest, iRisk, Agentic AI) but not explicitly documented. The RFT questions Zycus is answering likely encode Bank Rakyat's functional, technical, and commercial criteria (source-to-pay workflow coverage, contract intelligence, supplier risk, AI/automation capability, APAC bank reference cases, InfoSec/compliance, pricing model, implementation timeline), but the WEIGHTING, the evaluation committee composition, and the shortlist threshold are unknown. Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c fields returned no data. The Next Step updates confirm RFT submission is underway with partner support but do not name specific decision criteria. Without knowing whether the buyer prioritizes cost vs capability, AI innovation vs proven workflow stability, or incumbent-integration ease vs greenfield speed, Zycus cannot tailor the response to the highest-weighted criteria. The confirmed criterion is multi-module S2P coverage (eight products in scope). The recommended move is to request the RFT evaluation matrix or scoring rubric from the partner or Syiadatul before final submission to align the response and demo strategy.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

blocks.read: We're well behind — this one is cold.
blocks.title: Zycus win position

bullets.text: With zero MEDDPICC strength (no EB, no champion, no pain, no competitive intel, all gaps or partial), the rubric adjustment is deeply negative and cannot lift the score
Single contact (Syiadatul, Procurement Executive, Evaluator role) with no advocacy, no executive access, and no evidence she is championing Zycus internally.; Champion factor scores as missing

No competitor identified in a formal eight-module RFT at a Malaysian bank.; SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Oracle, and regional/incumbent players are almost certainly competing.; This blind spot means we cannot claim competitive position strength
No quantified pain, business case, or ROI documented.; The RFT implies organizational need, but we have not discovered or validated the specific problem we solve or the value we deliver

blocks.read: Going quiet — engagement has dropped off.
blocks.title: Deal momentum

bullets.text: Zero buyer meetings ever recorded and zero email touches in 60 days.; The only activity is rep-side Next Step updates; no bidirectional conversation exists in the datalake
RFT invitation (rep-noted, unverified by meeting or buyer email) would normally score as tier-6 engagement, but with no buyer interaction to validate it, the engagement tier collapses to near-zero
Original RFP deadline was 15 June; we are now 11 days past with no documented extension date, revised timeline, or buyer confirmation.; The deal may have moved forward without us
Qualified stage expects buyer touch every 30 days.; With no meeting or inbound email on record, the stall penalty is severe; roughly 25 points off baseline momentum

blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.
blocks.title: What could lose it

blocks.title: What moves it forward

contributions.detail: RFP deadline was 15 June; we are now 11 days overdue with no documented extension, revised submission date, or shortlist notification. The deal may have progressed to evaluation without our knowledge.
Zero competitive intelligence in a multi-vendor RFT. We do not know the shortlist, the incumbent, peer vendor positioning, or pricing benchmarks. We are flying blind into a contested evaluation.

Only one buyer contact (Evaluator role, no decision authority) and no Economic Buyer or Decision Maker engagement. Partner is building BOD relationship externally, but no internal sponsor exists to navigate the evaluation.
No quantified pain or business case. If the buyer deprioritizes AI or selects on cost, we have no validated wedge to defend our position or justify our price.
Complete buyer silence: zero meetings, zero emails in 60 days. The datalake shows no evidence the buyer is engaging with us at all. This is the highest-risk signal in the entire packet.

items.name: Syiadatul Nazlia Mohd Hassim
items.risk: Single point of contact. No evidence of advocacy or Economic Buyer access. If she deprioritizes or leaves, deal stalls.
items.title: Procurement Executive

items.detail: Only one buyer-side contact role (Syiadatul, Procurement Executive, Evaluator). No engagement with Economic Buyer (CFO candidate), Decision Maker (EVP Procurement candidate), IT, Strategy, or Risk stakeholders. Single-threaded deals are at high risk of disqualification if the one contact leaves, deprioritizes, or cannot advocate across functions.

Zero competitive intelligence. No competitor identified, no knowledge of RFT shortlist, incumbent vendor, or peer vendor positioning (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP likely in APAC bank tenders). Submitting a blind RFT response without competitive context limits differentiation and increases risk of being out-positioned on pricing, references, or incumbent advantage.

No quantified pain, business problem, or ROI documented. The RFT implies organizational need, but the specific pain (manual procurement processes, incumbent system failure, compliance/risk gap, cost reduction target) and pain owner are unknown. Without a validated business case, the RFT response may miss the highest-weighted evaluation criteria and fail to resonate with Economic Buyer and Strategy stakeholders.

Original RFP submission deadline was 15 June (now 11 days past). Current status of deadline extension, revised submission date, evaluation timeline, shortlist notification, and award date are unknown. Timeline uncertainty creates risk of missed milestones and limits ability to plan next steps (demo, pricing, commercial negotiation).

Agentic AI is in product scope but has not been validated as a decision criterion or landed with the buyer. The risk is that AI is a checkbox in the RFT rather than a differentiated wedge. Without a tailored AI use case (contract intelligence, supplier risk, negotiation automation) and demo, Zycus may lose on price to a traditional competitor who positions 'good enough' workflow automation at lower cost.

Syiadatul Nazlia Mohd Hassim (Procurement Executive, Evaluator) is the sole buyer-side contact with no confirmed advocacy, no Economic Buyer access, and no evidence of internal sponsorship. Partner is building BOD relationship externally (bumiputra partner, 2026-07-01) but no internal champion confirmed.

items.action: Request partner introduce Zycus to Economic Buyer (CFO candidate or EVP Procurement) to validate budget and align RFT response to board priorities.
items.trigger: No Economic Buyer engagement confirmed. OpportunityContactRole shows only Syiadatul (Procurement Executive, Evaluator). CFO and EVP Procurement candidates exist in account contacts but unengaged. RFT submission second half July (imminent), and EB validation needed before final submission to de-risk budget approval and align commercial proposal.
items.expected_effect: Validates budget availability, aligns RFT pricing/ROI to CFO priorities, de-risks final approval objections, and creates path to champion development at Economic Buyer level.

items.action: Surface RFT shortlist, incumbent vendor name, and evaluation criteria weighting from Syiadatul or partner before final submission.
items.trigger: Zero competitive intelligence. No competitor identified, shortlist unknown, incumbent unconfirmed. RFT submission imminent (second half July per 2026-07-01 Next Step). Without competitive context, cannot differentiate response or price strategically.
items.expected_effect: Enables tailored RFT response to highest-weighted criteria, positions Agentic AI as wedge vs named competitors, prices competitively vs shortlist, and surfaces incumbent integration/reference advantage to counter.

items.action: Add buyer-side contact roles to opportunity for IT Head, Chief Strategy Officer, EVP Procurement, and Risk Head to establish multi-thread baseline.
items.trigger: Only 1 buyer-side OpportunityContactRole (Syiadatul, Procurement Executive). Account contacts include IT Head (Khalsom Mohd Ali), Chief Strategy Officer (Firdaus Faudzi), EVP Procurement (Mohd Shaifuddin Abdullah), Risk Head (Norhayati Maamor), but none are linked to opportunity. Single-thread exposes deal to disqualification if Syiadatul deprioritizes or leaves.
items.expected_effect: Creates multi-thread visibility, signals cross-functional engagement to Salesforce, and enables partner to route Zycus to IT/Strategy/Risk stakeholders for technical/business validation beyond Procurement.

items.action: Request discovery call with the relevant stakeholder or CFO candidate through partner to surface quantified pain and ROI target before RFT submission.
items.trigger: No quantified business case, pain, or ROI documented. Customer_Business_Problem__c, Value_to_Customer__c returned no data. RFT response risks missing highest-weighted evaluation criteria without validated pain/ROI anchored to board priorities.
items.expected_effect: Anchors RFT response to board-level pain/ROI priorities, tailors Agentic AI narrative to confirmed use case (contract intelligence, supplier risk, negotiation automation), and strengthens evaluation score on business case criteria.

items.action: Confirm revised RFT submission deadline, evaluation timeline, shortlist notification date, and award date from Syiadatul or partner.
items.trigger: Original RFP deadline 15 June slipped to second half July (2026-07-01 Next Step), now 20 days past. Current submission date, evaluation duration, shortlist date, award date unknown. Cannot plan demo/commercial/legal resources or pressure-test close date without timeline.
items.expected_effect: Enables resource planning for demo/pricing/InfoSec rounds, pressure-tests 2026-12-31 close date credibility, and surfaces risk of further slippage to Q1 2027.

items: Partner building BOD relationship with bumiputra partner (2026-07-01 Next Step) signals active strategic positioning
Vendor registration completed (2026-05-20) confirms Zycus is qualified to bid
RFT questions being answered signals deal team engagement and submission in progress
No buyer-direct engagement (zero Avoma calls, single contact role, no new stakeholder additions since creation) limits confidence in deal health visibility
RFP deadline slip from 15 June to second half July (20 days past original date) signals buyer-side delay but not cancellation
Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-13 (22 days ago) is stale but not dark for a partner-led tender cycle
confidence_signals.summary: Partner-led channel with procedural momentum (vendor registration complete, RFT response in progress, BOD relationship building) but no buyer-direct engagement visibility. No Avoma calls, single buyer contact, no champion, no EB access, no competitive intel. Confidence is Low due to thin evidence and partner-mediated discovery gaps, but deal is not cooling (active partner work, recent Next Step update 2026-07-01, 22 days since last Salesforce activity is normal for partner-led RFT prep).

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-05-19 (47 days ago), in Qualified stage since creation. Last Salesforce activity 2026-06-13 (22 days ago). RFT submission original deadline 2026-06-15 slipped to second half of July 2026 per 2026-07-01 Next Step update. 179 days to close 2026-12-31. Typical enterprise bank procurement cycle (RFT submission → evaluation → shortlist → demos → commercials → InfoSec → contracting) runs 9-12 months. No submission-to-shortlisted date series captured. Even with expedited bank procurement, RFT evaluation (4-6 weeks), shortlist/demos (4-8 weeks), commercials (4-6 weeks), and contracting (6-8 weeks) cannot complete by year-end from late July start. Date will slip to Q1 2027.
evidence: Next Step 2026-07-01: RFP dates delayed, expected second half July. Building BOD relationship with bumiputra partner. 75% confidence.
Next Step 2026-06-01: Confirmed RFP due by 15 June. Good relationships built, drip feed BFSI data points.
Next Step 2026-05-20: Responded to vendor management/procurement with Zycus details. Registration complete. Await tender mid/late June.
No completed Tasks beyond registration. No shortlist date, no demo scheduled, no EB/DM engagement.
Zero buyer calls in Avoma discovery (partner-led channel). Single buyer contact role (Syiadatul, Procurement Executive, Evaluator).
Pipeline forecast with no champion, no EB access, no competitive read, and 179 days to navigate full bank procurement cycle is indefensible.
north_star_verdict.headline: RFT submission in progress with partner channel support, but no buyer engagement mapped, no competitive intelligence, and 179 days to close with deep discovery, shortlist, demos, commercials, and contracting still ahead. The deal is fundamentally healthy given active RFT response work and partner momentum, but the close date 2026-12-31 is not credible on current pace.

flags: Extract RFT evaluation criteria weights and tailor submission to highest-scoring dimensions (technical fit, AI/Agentic capability, BFSI reference strength, pricing model, regional support). Position Zycus Agentic AI as a differentiated modernization lever vs traditional competitors, and provide tier-1 Malaysian or APAC bank references to de-risk vendor selection.
best_practice_check.summary: Partner-led RFT response in progress with procedural momentum, but the deal lacks strategic discovery: no Economic Buyer access, no champion advocacy, zero competitive intelligence, single-threaded to a Procurement Evaluator, and no quantified business case or validated AI wedge. High risk of blind RFT submission that fails to differentiate on weighted criteria, gets out-positioned on price by a competitor with incumbent advantage or better EB alignment, or scores low on AI/references/compliance. Best-practice correction: multi-thread to senior stakeholders (CFO, EVP Procurement, Strategy Officer) through the partner to validate pain/ROI, surface the shortlist and evaluation weighting, develop a champion with EB access, and tailor the Agentic AI narrative to a confirmed use case before final submission.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer calls, no buyer-stated preference in Next Step updates or Tasks. Partner building BOD relationship signals positioning work, but no direct buyer preference articulated.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has been identified. In a formal RFT for an 8-module S2P suite at a Malaysian bank, multiple vendors are almost certainly competing (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua, Oracle are typical APAC enterprise procurement peers, plus potential regional/incumbent players). The absence of competitive intelligence is a blind spot: we do not know the shortlist, the incumbent (if any), their pricing, or their positioning, which limits our ability to differentiate in the RFT response. The immediate action is to extract competitive intel from the tender process and from Syiadatul or other contacts. Current threat level unknown due to zero visibility.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Agentic AI is in product scope but unvalidated as a decision criterion, use case, or differentiated wedge. No AI-specific discovery, demo, or ROI workshop documented. Risk: AI becomes a checkbox the buyer deprioritizes on cost vs a competitor's proven workflow automation. Under-positioned.

items.criterion: Multi-module S2P platform coverage (contract, source, supplier, procurement, invoicing, risk)

items.quote: Agentic AI in product scope, but no validated AI use case, demo, or ROI justification with buyer
items.source: SF Products__c, Next Step updates (no AI validation mentioned)
items.criterion: Agentic AI capability for procurement automation

items.quote: No references, InfoSec, or compliance requirements documented. APAC bank RFTs typically mandate regional references and data sovereignty compliance.
items.source: Inference from APAC bank tender norms, no Salesforce/Next Step confirmation
items.criterion: APAC bank reference cases and regulatory compliance (PDPA Malaysia, central bank audit)

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Product scope (eight modules including Agentic AI) signals broad S2P/contract/sourcing platform need, but specific evaluation criteria, pain priorities, and AI use-case fit are unvalidated. Partial alignment inferred from RFT invitation and product scope; exposure risks on pricing, incumbent integration, APAC bank references, and AI ROI justification without tailored validation.

A blind RFT submission without competitive context is a missed opportunity to win on differentiation.

value.detail: Original RFP submission deadline was 15 June (now 20 days past). Current status of deadline extension, revised submission date, evaluation timeline, shortlist notification, and award date are unknown. Timeline uncertainty creates risk of missed milestones and limits ability to plan next steps (demo, pricing, commercial negotiation).

value.flag: Multi-thread urgently to Economic Buyer (CFO candidate from account contacts), Decision Maker (EVP Procurement candidate Mohd Shaifuddin Abdullah), IT Head (Khalsom Mohd Ali), and Chief Strategy Officer (Firdaus Faudzi) through the partner channel before RFT submission. Single-threading to Syiadatul (Procurement Executive, Evaluator) exposes Zycus to disqualification if she deprioritizes, leaves, or cannot advocate across functions, and blocks access to budget authority, technical sign-off, and board-level sponsorship needed to win a $200k APAC bank platform deal.

packets.subject: Multi-thread urgently to Economic Buyer (CFO candidate from account contacts), Decision Maker (EVP Procurement candidate Mohd Shaifuddin Abdullah), IT Head (Khalsom Mohd Ali), and Chief Strategy Officer (Firdaus Faudzi) through the partner channel before RFT submission. Single-threading to Syiadatul (Procurement Executive, Evaluator) exposes Zycus to disqualification if she deprioritizes, leaves, or cannot advocate across functions, and blocks access to budget authority, technical sign-off, and board-level sponsorship needed to win a $200k APAC bank platform deal.

value.flag: Surface the RFT shortlist, incumbent vendor name, and evaluation criteria weighting from Syiadatul or the partner before final submission. Submitting a blind RFT response without knowing whether Bank Rakyat prioritizes cost vs AI innovation, whether an incumbent has integration/reference advantage, or which peer vendors (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua) are competing limits Zycus's ability to differentiate on the highest-weighted criteria, price competitively, and position Agentic AI as the wedge rather than a checkbox.

packets.subject: Surface the RFT shortlist, incumbent vendor name, and evaluation criteria weighting from Syiadatul or the partner before final submission. Submitting a blind RFT response without knowing whether Bank Rakyat prioritizes cost vs AI innovation, whether an incumbent has integration/reference advantage, or which peer vendors (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua) are competing limits Zycus's ability to differentiate on the highest-weighted criteria, price competitively, and position Agentic AI as the wedge rather than a checkbox.

value.flag: Validate a quantified business case and pain owner with the Economic Buyer or Strategy lead before RFT submission. Without confirmed pain (manual contract cycle time, supplier risk blind spots, invoice processing cost, procurement compliance gap) and ROI target, the RFT response risks missing the evaluation criteria that matter most to the CFO and board, and Agentic AI remains an unproven capability rather than a tailored solution to their highest-priority problem. Request a discovery call with the CFO candidate or Chief Strategy Officer through the partner to anchor the submission to board-level priorities.
